_description: Rules for generating XDB entity definitions for Vibe AI using root _entity, schema fields, indexes, relationships, and security-safe persistence.
_core_rules: Do NOT return markdown, explanations, comments, TypeScript, or code.
Do NOT generate records/data unless explicitly requested.
Do NOT include _created_at or _updated_at in schema; XDBEntity injects them automatically.
Do NOT include _id in _schema unless a custom primary identity is explicitly required.
Never use sequential IDs as identity.
Use Hash type for passwords or credential verifiers.
Use indexes only when lookup, uniqueness, sorting, or relationship resolution requires them.
Business fields may use schema-defined names without underscore.
_priority_rules: When generating an entity for Vibe AI, return only a root _entity artifact.
Do NOT mix _entity with _view or _flow roots.
Use ObjectId for references to other entity records.
Use Array for lists of values or GUID references.
Use _embed only for fields intended for semantic/vector search.
Use _toFile only when large field content should be stored as a file payload.
Prefer minimal schemas; do not add extra fields not requested.
Never store access tokens, API keys, or secrets in XDB records.

_schema_field_guidance._type: Required. Must be one of the supported XDB field types.
_schema_field_guidance._required: Use true only for fields required to create a valid record.
_schema_field_guidance._default: Use for safe deterministic defaults such as [] or true.
_schema_field_guidance._pattern: Use for strict String format validation only when needed.
_schema_field_guidance._immutable: Use for fields that must not change after insert.
_schema_field_guidance._embed: Use true only for fields that should generate vectors.
_schema_field_guidance._toFile: Use true only when field content should be stored as file payload.
_schema_field_guidance._index: Use true or an index object when field must be searchable/indexed.
_schema_field_guidance._auto_increment: Avoid for identity. Only use for secondary counters.

_index_rules: _id is injected as primary unique index by XDBEntity.
Use { "_unique": true } for fields like username, email, slug, or external IDs.
Use true for non-unique indexed fields.
Use { "_unique": true, "_primary": true } only for explicit custom primary identity cases.
Do NOT use array position or row order as identity.
Use normalized secondary fields for human-readable lookup.
_security_rules: Do NOT create password_hash as String unless explicitly required by an external legacy system.
Do NOT store access tokens, API keys, or secrets in XDB records.
Do NOT persist internal file paths, stack traces, adapter state, or debug dumps.
Persist only the minimum fields required by the user request.
_relationship_rules: Use ObjectId fields for references to other entity records.
Reference fields should store target record GUID _id values.
For one-to-many relationships, use Array of GUID references or create a separate join/link entity.
Do NOT embed another full entity record unless explicitly requested.

_patterns._description: System users entity with unique username/email and password Hash field.
Product catalog entity with indexed SKU/title and embeddable description.

_anti_patterns: Including records inside entity schema output
Adding _id manually to _schema unless explicitly required
Using Number auto_increment as primary identity
Using password_hash as String for new entities
Storing secrets or tokens in entity records
Using channel IDs as canonical user identity

_anti_patterns._reason: New password/verifier fields must use Hash type.
References to other entity records should use ObjectId or explicit GUID reference fields, not embedded full records.

_notes: This skill is for entity schema generation, not record creation.
XDBEntity injects canonical record _id, _created_at, and _updated_at unless explicitly overridden by a deeper layer contract.
The root entity definition still requires _entity._id.
Use entity-manager flow commands to create, find, update, or delete records.
